name: Swap_Mech

infobox.Systems: Teleporter(Science)EffectSwap places with a nearby tile.Upgrade I+1 Range ()Extends range of the swap by 1 tile.Upgrade II+2 Range ()Extends range of the swap by 2 tiles.
infobox.Special: Flying: Flying units can move over any terrain tile.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theFlame Behemothssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Swap Mech is a very versatile Mech that relies on manipulating unit positions, but relies on getting upgraded Teleporter range to maximize its potential. Understanding how to best take advantage of Teleporter is vital, examples including:
  - Ways of turning Vek attacks against their own by swapping with them; before choosing targets to swap with, check the Attack Order.
  - Positioning the Swap Mech above environmental hazards, such as water, lava, or acid; and swapping with a grounded Vek.
  - Swap with allies or empty spaces to eke out extra squares of movement, or escape webbing.
Upgrading the Range of its Teleporter can give you a large advantage in the mid and late game; the first upgrade for+1 Rangeonly costs 1 Reactor Core, and is recommended first. Once the Swap Mech gets a 2nd Reactor core, you can disable the first upgrade, and reallocate its Reactor Core with the new one into+2 Range. Maximum range is ideal to have by the end of the game, particularly in a 3 or 4 island run.
Placing your previous Time Traveler, preferably with an extra Reactor Core ability, on the Swap Mech has a few economical benefits. The Swap Mech is unlikely to gain XP, which doesn't affect a leveled-up Time Traveler. The Swap Mech also starts with 0 Power, but with +1 Mech Reactor, the Swap Mech can be used to extend the Teleport range right away.
